intro: Hubera forbesii, also known as the Forb's Hubera, is a plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, characterized by its unique, fragrant flowers and edible fruits. The health benefits of Hubera forbesii include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Hubera forbesii include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to possess cytotoxic and anti-cancer properties, as well as annonin, a compound with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Hubera forbesii is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make extracts, tinctures, and capsules, which are valued for their potential therapeutic benefits.
